    <description>Captures learnings, errors, and corrections to enable continuous improvement. Also review learnings before major tasks.</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/self-improving-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

compactionCheckpoints.summary:
## Goal
- Maintain OpenClaw system health and stability
- Process daily briefings (AI早报/AI晚报/工作AI简报/OpenClaw日报) from cron-output and send via Feishu
- Keep system running at v2026.4.9 with daily Gateway restart at 6 AM

## Constraints & Preferences
- HEARTBEAT.md must be checked on every heartbeat (every 30 minutes)
- cron-output files: read content, send via Feishu to 王 (openId: ou_748c15a782356f78466b01436dbbcbe8), delete after sending; if content is "无新内容" or "本次推送 0 条" just delete without sending
- Use `accountId="main"` when sending feishu messages (default account "default" is not configured)

## Progress
### Done
- [x] OpenClaw upgraded from v2026.4.1 → v2026.4.5 → v2026.4.9, backup at `~/.openclaw/backups/20260406_2106/`
- [x] feishu-agent token overflow issue (replies=0 on multiple messages): Created daily Gateway restart LaunchAgent at `~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway-daily-restart.plist` (runs at 6:00 AM daily)
- [x] daily.html syntax error fixed: `selectedClass + '" data-id="' ` → `selectedClass + ' data-id="' ` (extra double-quote removed at byte position ~39982)
- [x] Project tracker restarted after daily Gateway restart
- [x] 故障记录.md updated with: 04-09 feishu-agent再次爆满、daily.html JS语法错误、定时重启设置
- [x] Memory commit: "feat: 记录04-09故障（feishu-agent再次爆满、定时重启、daily.html语法错误）"

### In Progress
- (none)

### Blocked
- (none)

## Key Decisions
- **Daily Gateway restart at 6 AM**: Resets all sessions including feishu-agent, prevents token accumulation. LaunchAgent `ai.openclaw.gateway-daily-restart.plist` handles this.
- **feishu-agent is not reliable for critical tasks**: When feishu-agent fails, main session handles via direct feishu API

## Next Steps
1. Continue heartbeat checks every 30 minutes
2. If feishu-agent fails again (replies=0), restart Gateway manually and verify daily restart LaunchAgent is working
3. Project tracker's `daily.html` needs real user testing to confirm edit button and toggle checkbox work

## Critical Context
- **feishu DM openId**: `ou_748c15a782356f78466b01436dbbcbe8`
- **Project tracker source**: `~/.openclaw/workspace/work/projects/templates/daily.html` (syntax error was at line ~1115)
- **Project tracker server**: Python at `~/.openclaw/workspace/work/projects/server.py`
- **Project tracker URL**: `http://localhost:8765/`
- **Gateway current**: v2026.4.9, pid 81840, LaunchAgent running
- **Daily restart plist**: `~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway-daily-restart.plist`
- **cron-output directory**: `~/.openclaw/workspace/memory/cron-output/`
- **故障记录**: `~/.openclaw/workspace/memory/topics/11-故障记录.md`
- **Memory sessions**: `~/.openclaw/workspace/memory/sessions/`

---

**Turn Context (split turn):**

## Original Request
User triggered a HEARTBEAT check at 10:09 PM Shanghai time, instructing the assistant to read HEARTBEAT.md from the workspace directory if it exists and follow it strictly.

## Early Progress
- Assistant checked the cron-output directory for new files
- Found `ai-evening-briefing.txt` (dated Apr 10 21:39)
- Read the evening AI briefing containing multiple news items about:
  - GPT-5, Gemini 3, Claude 4 model comparisons
  - Claude Mythos (new Anthropic model)
  - 2026 AI trends and tool rankings
  - Various AI tools and platform news

## Context for Suffix
- The briefing was truncated at 1514 characters remaining
- The full content includes additional AI news items and links that were cut off
- This is a standard evening briefing generated by cron, likely requiring acknowledgment or further action per HEARTBEAT.md instructions

## Goal
- Maintain OpenClaw system health and stability
- Process daily briefings (AI早报/AI晚报/工作AI简报/OpenClaw日报) from cron-output and send via Feishu
- Keep system running at v2026.4.9 with daily Gateway restart at 6 AM

## Constraints & Preferences
- HEARTBEAT.md must be checked on every heartbeat (every 30 minutes)
- cron-output files: read content, send via Feishu to 王 (openId: ou_748c15a782356f78466b01436dbbcbe8), delete after sending; if content is "无新内容" or "本次推送 0 条" just delete without sending
- Use `accountId="main"` when sending feishu messages
- AI早报 generates at ~08:30 Beijing time, OpenClaw日报 at ~09:30, AI工作简报 at ~12:00, AI晚报 at ~17:30

## Progress
### Done
- [x] OpenClaw upgraded from v2026.4.1 → v2026.4.5 → v2026.4.9, backup at `~/.openclaw/backups/20260406_2106/`
- [x] feishu-agent token overflow issue: Created daily Gateway restart LaunchAgent at `~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway-daily-restart.plist` (runs at 6:00 AM daily)
- [x] daily.html syntax error fixed
- [x] Project tracker restarted after daily Gateway restart
- [x] 故障记录.md updated with: 04-09 feishu-agent再次爆满、daily.html JS语法错误、定时重启设置
- [x] Memory commit: "feat: 记录04-09故障（feishu-agent再次爆满、定时重启、daily.html语法错误）"
- [x] Daily Gateway restarts verified working (Apr 11 pid 39113, Apr 12 pid 15895, Apr 14 pid 65077)
- [x] Multiple briefings processed and sent to Feishu (Apr 10-13)
- [x] VentureBeat AI news scraping via browser tool — 8 articles successfully extracted repeatedly (same set each time)
- [x] 机器之心 (jiqizhixin.com) limitation confirmed: article URLs loaded via JavaScript, not available in static HTML snapshot — attempted 6+ times with same result
- [x] Translated 16+ AI news articles from The Verge, VentureBeat, and 36kr (including duplicate articles re-translated)
- [x] All 7 heartbeat checks confirmed cron-output empty, no briefings to send
- [x] Morning briefings Apr 14-15 did not generate; no action needed

### In Progress
- [x] OpenClaw v2026.4.14 update available (not yet applied)

### Blocked
- [none]

## Key Decisions
- **Daily Gateway restart at 6 AM**: Resets all sessions including feishu-agent, prevents token accumulation
- **feishu-agent is not reliable for critical tasks**: When feishu-agent fails, main session handles via direct feishu API
- **Empty briefings**: AI早报 files with "0 条" or no content are deleted without sending to Feishu
- **Morning briefings not generating**: Apr 14-15 morning briefings did not appear in cron-output; no action needed
- **VentureBeat snapshot extraction**: Successfully extracts article URLs from static HTML — same 8 articles returned every time
- **机器之心 page limitation**: Consistent inability to extract article URLs from JS-rendered page — no workaround found with current tools

## Next Steps
1. Continue heartbeat checks every 30 minutes
2. Monitor if morning briefings resume on Apr 15+
3. Consider applying OpenClaw v2026.4.14 update if user requests
4. Project tracker's `daily.html` needs real user testing

## Critical Context
- **feishu DM openId**: `ou_748c15a782356f78466b01436dbbcbe8`
- **Project tracker source**: `~/.openclaw/workspace/work/projects/templates/daily.html`
- **Project tracker server**: Python at `~/.openclaw/workspace/work/projects/server.py`
- **Project tracker URL**: `http://localhost:8765/`
- **Gateway current**: v2026.4.9, pid 65077 (Apr 14 restart), LaunchAgent running, reachable ~35-47ms
- **Daily restart plist**: `~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway-daily-restart.plist`

- **故障记录**: `~/.openclaw/workspace/memory/topics/11-故障记录.md`
- **OpenClaw update available**: v2026.4.14 (not yet applied)

---

**Turn Context (split turn):**



## Original Request
Scheduled task to prepare an AI evening briefing for "王" - a Chinese AI news digest with strict source filtering (36kr, 机器之心, 雷峰网 preferred), max 10 items, Chinese titles/summaries, and archive to `~/.openclaw/workspace/memory/cron-history/ai-evening-briefing/今日日期.md`.

## Early Progress
1. Executed two tavily searches: "AI人工智能 最新新闻 2026" (10 results) and targeted 36kr/机器之心/雷峰网 search (8 results)
2. Created the archive directory
3. Search results received covering: Anthropic's Project Glasswing (cybersecurity), Google AI Overviews accuracy issues, Meta's Muse Spark model, AI agent adoption stats (96% enterprises), 36kr articles on 2026 AI trends

## Context for Suffix
The assistant has raw search results but hasn't yet processed/filtered them, selected top items, compiled the final briefing, or written to the archive file. The suffix contains recent work showing the assistant was likely processing the search results to compile the briefing.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

Also review learnings before major tasks.

resolvedSkills.description: Example ClawFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: clawhub
resolvedSkills.description: Use the ClawHub CLI to search, install, update, and publish agent skills from clawhub.com. Use when you need to fetch new skills on the fly, sync installed skills to latest or a specific version, or publish new/updated skill folders with the npm-installed clawhub CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

resolvedSkills.name: self-improvement
resolvedSkills.description: Captures learnings, errors, and corrections to enable continuous improvement. Use when: (1) A command or operation fails unexpectedly, (2) User corrects Claude ('No, that's wrong...', 'Actually...'), (3) User requests a capability that doesn't exist, (4) An external API or tool fails, (5) Claude realizes its knowledge is outdated or incorrect, (6) A better approach is discovered for a recurring task. Also review learnings before major tasks.

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect

Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator

Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow
resolvedSkills.description: Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. TaskFlow is the durable flow substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, ACPX, plugins, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example TaskFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

resolvedSkills.name: self-improvement
resolvedSkills.description: Captures learnings, errors, and corrections to enable continuous improvement. Use when: (1) A command or operation fails unexpectedly, (2) User corrects Claude ('No, that's wrong...', 'Actually...'), (3) User requests a capability that doesn't exist, (4) An external API or tool fails, (5) Claude realizes its knowledge is outdated or incorrect, (6) A better approach is discovered for a recurring task. Also review learnings before major tasks.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck

Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow
resolvedSkills.description: Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. TaskFlow is the durable flow substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, ACPX, plugins, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example TaskFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

resolvedSkills.name: self-improvement
resolvedSkills.description: Captures learnings, errors, and corrections to enable continuous improvement. Use when: (1) A command or operation fails unexpectedly, (2) User corrects Claude ('No, that's wrong...', 'Actually...'), (3) User requests a capability that doesn't exist, (4) An external API or tool fails, (5) Claude realizes its knowledge is outdated or incorrect, (6) A better approach is discovered for a recurring task. Also review learnings before major tasks.

resolvedSkills.description: Feishu knowledge base navigation. Activate when user mentions knowledge base, wiki, or wiki links.

resolvedSkills.name: tavily
resolvedSkills.description: Tavily web search, content extraction, and research tools.

resolvedSkills.name: clawhub
resolvedSkills.description: Use the ClawHub CLI to search, install, update, and publish agent skills from clawhub.com. Use when you need to fetch new skills on the fly, sync installed skills to latest or a specific version, or publish new/updated skill folders with the npm-installed clawhub CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow
resolvedSkills.description: Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. TaskFlow is the durable flow substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, ACPX, plugins, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example TaskFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

resolvedSkills.name: self-improvement
resolvedSkills.description: Captures learnings, errors, and corrections to enable continuous improvement. Use when: (1) A command or operation fails unexpectedly, (2) User corrects Claude ('No, that's wrong...', 'Actually...'), (3) User requests a capability that doesn't exist, (4) An external API or tool fails, (5) Claude realizes its knowledge is outdated or incorrect, (6) A better approach is discovered for a recurring task. Also review learnings before major tasks.

resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow
resolvedSkills.description: Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. TaskFlow is the durable flow substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, ACPX, plugins, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example TaskFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

resolvedSkills.name: self-improvement
resolvedSkills.description: Captures learnings, errors, and corrections to enable continuous improvement. Use when: (1) A command or operation fails unexpectedly, (2) User corrects Claude ('No, that's wrong...', 'Actually...'), (3) User requests a capability that doesn't exist, (4) An external API or tool fails, (5) Claude realizes its knowledge is outdated or incorrect, (6) A better approach is discovered for a recurring task. Also review learnings before major tasks.

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect

Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow
resolvedSkills.description: Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. TaskFlow is the durable flow substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, ACPX, plugins, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example TaskFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example TaskFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

resolvedSkills.name: self-improvement
resolvedSkills.description: Captures learnings, errors, and corrections to enable continuous improvement. Use when: (1) A command or operation fails unexpectedly, (2) User corrects Claude ('No, that's wrong...', 'Actually...'), (3) User requests a capability that doesn't exist, (4) An external API or tool fails, (5) Claude realizes its knowledge is outdated or incorrect, (6) A better approach is discovered for a recurring task. Also review learnings before major tasks.

resolvedSkills.name: feishu-drive
resolvedSkills.description: Feishu cloud storage file management. Activate when user mentions cloud space, folders, drive.

resolvedSkills.name: feishu-perm
resolvedSkills.description: Feishu permission management for documents and files. Activate when user mentions sharing, permissions, collaborators.

resolvedSkills.name: feishu-wiki
resolvedSkills.description: Feishu knowledge base navigation. Activate when user mentions knowledge base, wiki, or wiki links.

resolvedSkills.name: tavily
resolvedSkills.description: Tavily web search, content extraction, and research tools.

resolvedSkills.name: clawhub
resolvedSkills.description: Use the ClawHub CLI to search, install, update, and publish agent skills from clawhub.com. Use when you need to fetch new skills on the fly, sync installed skills to latest or a specific version, or publish new/updated skill folders with the npm-installed clawhub CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow
resolvedSkills.description: Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. TaskFlow is the durable flow substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, ACPX, plugins, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example TaskFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

resolvedSkills.name: self-improvement
resolvedSkills.description: Captures learnings, errors, and corrections to enable continuous improvement. Use when: (1) A command or operation fails unexpectedly, (2) User corrects Claude ('No, that's wrong...', 'Actually...'), (3) User requests a capability that doesn't exist, (4) An external API or tool fails, (5) Claude realizes its knowledge is outdated or incorrect, (6) A better approach is discovered for a recurring task. Also review learnings before major tasks.

resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow
resolvedSkills.description: Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. TaskFlow is the durable flow substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, ACPX, plugins, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example TaskFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

resolvedSkills.name: self-improvement
resolvedSkills.description: Captures learnings, errors, and corrections to enable continuous improvement. Use when: (1) A command or operation fails unexpectedly, (2) User corrects Claude ('No, that's wrong...', 'Actually...'), (3) User requests a capability that doesn't exist, (4) An external API or tool fails, (5) Claude realizes its knowledge is outdated or incorrect, (6) A better approach is discovered for a recurring task. Also review learnings before major tasks.

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect

Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow
resolvedSkills.description: Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. TaskFlow is the durable flow substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, ACPX, plugins, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example TaskFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example TaskFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

resolvedSkills.name: self-improvement
resolvedSkills.description: Captures learnings, errors, and corrections to enable continuous improvement. Use when: (1) A command or operation fails unexpectedly, (2) User corrects Claude ('No, that's wrong...', 'Actually...'), (3) User requests a capability that doesn't exist, (4) An external API or tool fails, (5) Claude realizes its knowledge is outdated or incorrect, (6) A better approach is discovered for a recurring task. Also review learnings before major tasks.

resolvedSkills.name: feishu-drive
resolvedSkills.description: Feishu cloud storage file management. Activate when user mentions cloud space, folders, drive.

resolvedSkills.name: feishu-perm
resolvedSkills.description: Feishu permission management for documents and files. Activate when user mentions sharing, permissions, collaborators.

resolvedSkills.name: feishu-wiki
resolvedSkills.description: Feishu knowledge base navigation. Activate when user mentions knowledge base, wiki, or wiki links.

resolvedSkills.name: tavily
resolvedSkills.description: Tavily web search, content extraction, and research tools.

resolvedSkills.name: clawhub
resolvedSkills.description: Use the ClawHub CLI to search, install, update, and publish agent skills from clawhub.com. Use when you need to fetch new skills on the fly, sync installed skills to latest or a specific version, or publish new/updated skill folders with the npm-installed clawhub CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow
resolvedSkills.description: Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. TaskFlow is the durable flow substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, ACPX, plugins, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example TaskFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

resolvedSkills.name: self-improvement
resolvedSkills.description: Captures learnings, errors, and corrections to enable continuous improvement. Use when: (1) A command or operation fails unexpectedly, (2) User corrects Claude ('No, that's wrong...', 'Actually...'), (3) User requests a capability that doesn't exist, (4) An external API or tool fails, (5) Claude realizes its knowledge is outdated or incorrect, (6) A better approach is discovered for a recurring task. Also review learnings before major tasks.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.
